feed.title: Example Developer Blog
feed.subtitle: Thoughts on software development and functional programming

entry.title: Railway Oriented Programming in F#
entry.summary: An elegant approach to error handling using Result types and function composition.
entry.content:
Railway Oriented Programming is a functional approach to error handling that makes your code more robust and easier to reason about.

Instead of throwing exceptions, we use the Result type to represent success or failure.

entry.title: Type Providers: Compile-Time Magic
entry.summary: How F# type providers generate types from external data sources at compile time.
entry.content: Type providers are one of F#'s most powerful features, allowing you to work with external data in a type-safe way.

entry.title: Computation Expressions Demystified
entry.summary: Understanding async, task, and custom computation expressions in F#.
entry.content: Computation expressions (also known as workflows) provide a clean syntax for working with monadic operations.
